text.title: Testing in JavaScript / TypeScript Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when testing-javascript is applied incorrectly. Each entry has a symptom (`description`), root cause (`reason`), and corrective fix (in `note`).

antipattern.description: `SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module` only in tests.
antipattern.reason: Jest CommonJS runtime against ESM-first source; bundler is Vite but runner is Jest.
antipattern.note: Fix: Switch to Vitest (it shares Vite config) or add `@swc/jest` transform with explicit ESM support.

antipattern.description: Component test passes locally, hangs in CI.
antipattern.reason: `waitFor` with default 1000ms timeout; CI is slow. Or leaked fake timer.
antipattern.note: Fix: Use `findByRole` (configurable timeout) and ensure `vi.useRealTimers()` runs in `afterEach`.

antipattern.description: MSW handler matches but request still hits real network.
antipattern.reason: `server.listen()` called after the component already issued the request (bad lifecycle order).
antipattern.note: Fix: Move `server.listen()` to `beforeAll`; reset between tests with `server.resetHandlers()`.

antipattern.description: Coverage stuck at 60% even with passing tests.
antipattern.reason: Coverage instrumentation excludes node_modules but also excludes generated TS via path patterns.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add explicit `coverage.include` pattern; verify with `vitest run --coverage` locally.
